metadata.description: Chinese vocabulary for activities, chatting, and returning home

content.name: to_have_fun_play
content.side_f: I want to go to the library to have fun.
content.side_g: 玩(儿) is colloquial and broad: 'play,' 'hang out,' or 'have fun.' The 儿 suffix (érhuà) is typical of northern/Beijing speech and often dropped in the south or in writing. Common collocations: 好玩 (fun), 出去玩 (go out to have fun), 玩游戏 (play games).

content.name: dynamic_particle
content.side_g: 了 is a high-frequency aspect/modal particle. Post-verb 了 signals a completed/realized action (我吃了饭); sentence-final 了 signals a changed state or new situation (下雨了). Don't equate it with English past tense — 我每天七点回家 has no 了 even though it's habitual. Tone is neutral.

content.name: library
content.side_f: The library has a lot of books.
content.side_g: 图书馆 literally 'picture-book-building' (图书 = books/publications, 馆 = hall/venue). 馆 is a productive suffix for public venues: 博物馆 (museum), 餐馆 (restaurant), 咖啡馆 (café). Neutral register, used in speech and writing alike.

content.name: together
content.side_g: 一起 is an adverb meaning 'together; alongside.' It precedes the verb: 我们一起吃饭 (we eat together), not after. Often paired with 和/跟 (和朋友一起去). Contrast with 一块儿 (yíkuàir), a colloquial near-synonym more common in the north.

content.name: to_chat
content.side_g: 聊天(儿) is a verb-object (离合词/separable verb): the object 天 can be split from 聊 by measures or aspect markers — 聊了一会儿天, 聊了很久的天. Cannot take a further object directly; say 跟他聊天, not 聊天他. Casual/neutral register.

content.name: chat
content.side_e: Wǒ gēn tā liáo le hěn jiǔ.
content.side_f: I chatted with him for a long time.
content.side_g: 聊 alone is a transitive/intransitive verb meaning 'chat casually,' lighter than 说话 or 谈. Unlike 聊天, it can take explicit objects/topics: 聊工作, 聊这个问题. Common collocations: 聊一聊, 聊一下, 好好聊聊. Informal register.

content.name: sky
content.side_f: Today the sky is very bright.
content.side_g: 天 has multiple senses: 'sky' (天很蓝), 'day' (三天, 今天, 每天), and 'weather/heaven' (天气, 老天). As a measure of days it is a 'bare' noun — no 个: 三天 not 三个天. In 聊天, 天 is semantically bleached toward 'topic/matter of the day.'

content.name: not_until_only_then
content.side_e: Wǒ wǎnshang jiǔ diǎn cái huí jiā.
content.side_f: I didn't go home until nine at night. (lit. 'nine o'clock only-then return home')
content.side_g: 才 marks that something happens later/less/with more difficulty than expected; no 了 follows in this 'not until' pattern (九点才回家, not 九点才回家了). Contrast with 就 (jiù), which marks 'as early/easily as' (九点就回家了 = 'already home by nine'). Also used for 'only' in amounts: 才五块钱.

content.name: to_go_home
content.side_e: Wǒ měi tiān liù diǎn huí jiā.
content.side_f: I go home at six every day.
content.side_g: 回家 is a verb-object compound (回 'return' + 家 'home'); 家 here is bare — no 到 or possessive needed. Separable: 回了一趟家, 回不了家. For 'go back to [other place]' use 回 + place: 回学校, 回中国, 回宿舍.

content.name: to_return
content.side_f: I want to return to the dorm.
content.side_g: 回 means 'return to somewhere one belongs/came from' — directional, unlike 去 (go to) or 来 (come). Takes a place directly: 回北京, 回学校, 回国. Also used as a measure for occurrences (这回, 下一回 = this/next time) and in 回答 (reply) and 回来/回去 (directional complements).
